objective: Define the Phase 1 distribution contract for org-level governance tooling under parent epic #288.
scope_boundary: Create an issue-backed PDCAR and structured plan for Phase 1.
Add the org governance tooling distribution runbook.
Add a machine-readable governance tooling package manifest.
Define version pinning, managed-file boundaries, repo-local override policy, compatibility expectations, verification surfaces, and rollback contract.
Keep downstream e2e proof as the final acceptance criterion for parent epic #288.
Do not implement the Phase 2 deployer in this slice.
out_of_scope: Changing Local CI Gate runner behavior.
Weakening existing hardgates or CI requirements.
Treating this contract as downstream adoption proof.
research_summary: Issue #288 established that governance tooling should be consumed from a pinned hldpro-governance package instead of recreated per repo. Existing surfaces include Local CI Gate runner/profiles, managed shim deployer, execution-scope validation, structured-plan validation, workflow coverage inventory, graphify hook helpers, and closeout evidence conventions. Phase 1 turns those into a documented and machine-readable distribution contract while leaving deployment implementation and downstream pilot proof to later child slices.

sprints.name: Planning And Scope
sprints.goal: Create issue-backed planning and execution-scope evidence before contract edits.
tasks: Create PDCAR, structured plan, cross-review memo, exception, and execution scope.
Declare active parallel sibling lanes without cleaning them.
acceptance_criteria: Execution scope preflight passes from the isolated worktree.

sprints.name: Distribution Contract
sprints.goal: Define the package contract and runbook for reusable governance tooling.
tasks: Classify reusable surfaces as package core, repo profile, managed file, repo-local override, tracked baseline, or per-run report.
Add the operator runbook for consuming, verifying, and rolling back shared tooling.
Specify Phase 2 deployer requirements and Phase 4/5 downstream proof gates.
acceptance_criteria: The runbook names included and excluded surfaces.
The manifest is valid JSON and machine-readable.
Every shared tool in the manifest has an owner, version rule, consumer mode, and verification command.
Managed-file and override rules are explicit.
Rollback/uninstall contract is specific enough for Phase 2 implementation.
Final downstream e2e remains mandatory for #288 closure.

sprints.name: Validation And Closeout
sprints.goal: Prove the contract artifacts validate locally and in GitHub before closing issue #290.
tasks: Run JSON validation for the package manifest and structured plan.
Run structured plan validation and backlog alignment.
Open PR, watch GitHub Actions, and close issue #290 only after checks pass.
acceptance_criteria: Closeout records evidence and keeps #288 open.
Issue #290 closes via PR; #288 remains open for Phase 2-5.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify package-core candidates, repo-profile candidates, generated/report artifacts, managed shim surfaces, and validation risks.
specialist_reviews.summary: Subagent review informed the manifest/runbook surface classification and recommended keeping deployment implementation out of Phase 1.

alternate_model_review.summary: True alternate-family review was unavailable in this Codex lane. Same-family exception is scoped to contract documentation and manifest only, mitigated by subagent review, deterministic validation, Local CI Gate, and GitHub Actions.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #290 may add the Phase 1 distribution contract runbook, package manifest, planning/review/scope artifacts, status mirrors, and closeout evidence. It may not implement deployer code or edit downstream repos.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Validate, PR, watch checks, close issue #290, and leave parent epic #288 open for Phase 2.

material_deviation_rules: If deployer code changes are needed, stop and create the Phase 2 issue first.
If downstream repo edits are needed, stop and create a downstream issue and execution scope first.
If the contract permits unpinned consumption, stop and revise it.
If rollback is not represented, do not close issue #290.
If final downstream e2e proof is implied complete, stop and revise because #288 must remain open.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules and loop through PDCAR, GH epic, ACs, e2e testing as final AC
